skill.name: skill-management
skill.description: Create, update, test, and manage Claude and Codex agent skills, including local skill structure and team sharing workflows.
skill.summary: Guide agents through creating, editing, testing, and organizing reusable AI skills.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many markdown backticks, hidden .claude paths, and a removal command. Most backtick and weak-crypto alerts are false positives from documentation examples, but Bash/Edit/Write permissions and filesystem-changing examples create a legitimate medium-risk publishing concern.

medium_findings.title: Broad Tool Permissions Enable File and Shell Changes
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash, Edit, and Write access while guiding users through skill creation and maintenance. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it allows filesystem modifications and shell commands when the skill is active.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permission list is explicit and directly supports shell and file modification. The intent appears administrative rather than malicious, so the finding is medium severity.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Modification Examples Need User Review
medium_findings.description: The documentation includes commands that create, inspect, edit, and remove skill directories under personal and project .claude paths. The examples are expected for skill management, but users should review paths before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands and .claude paths are present in markdown examples. They are not hidden behavior, but they could modify or delete user files if copied with incorrect paths.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: The static external-command alerts come from markdown code fences, inline code, and shell examples. No executable Ruby source file or runtime backtick execution was found in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside markdown examples and structural documentation. I found no evidence that the skill executes Ruby or dynamically evaluates user input.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts correspond to ordinary words in descriptions, examples, and checklist text. No evidence found of cryptographic code, hash use, or encryption logic in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file contains no cryptographic implementation. The static analyzer appears to have matched substrings such as description text rather than crypto APIs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Delete Command in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows rm -rf commands for removing personal and project skills. The paths are scoped to skill directories, but recursive deletion should be confirmed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The recursive deletion command is directly present. The command is scoped to example skill directories, so it is risky documentation rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: AI skill authors need consistent structure, metadata, and testing habits. This skill provides practical guidance for creating, editing, sharing, and maintaining agent skills.
actual_capabilities: Explains where personal, project, and plugin skills are stored.
Shows the required SKILL.md frontmatter fields and naming rules.
Provides examples for creating, listing, editing, and removing skills.
Describes how to share project skills through Git workflows.
Gives troubleshooting checks for skill discovery and YAML issues.
Outlines best practices for focused skills and concise descriptions.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not validate skills automatically.
Examples assume Claude-style skill directories and may need adaptation.
Some commands can modify or delete local files if used incorrectly.
It does not replace marketplace security review or package validation.

use_cases.title: Create a personal skill
use_cases.description: Build a new local skill with correct directory structure, frontmatter, and concise instructions.

use_cases.title: Standardize team skills
use_cases.description: Add project skills to a repository so teammates can share consistent AI workflows.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot skill activation
use_cases.description: Check descriptions, file names, YAML syntax, and scope when a skill is not being used.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a simple personal skill for my workflow. Include the folder path, SKILL.md fields, and a short first draft.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a skill description
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill description and make it clearer about what the skill does and when it should be used.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a team skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through adding a project skill to our repository, including structure, review checks, and Git steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a skill library
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my skill library organization and identify duplicate scopes, unclear descriptions, missing examples, and risky tool permissions.

output_examples.input: I want a skill for writing release notes from merged pull requests.
output_examples.output: A recommended skill name, a concise description, required tool guidance, and a short instruction outline.

output_examples.input: My project skill is not being selected by Claude Code.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering description triggers, file naming, YAML validity, and project path placement.

output_examples.input: We need to share three skills across our engineering team.
output_examples.output: A repository layout plan, review steps, and a practical Git workflow for team rollout.

best_practices: Keep each skill focused on one workflow or decision area.
Write descriptions that state the task and the exact usage trigger.
Review tool permissions before sharing a skill with a team.
anti_patterns: Do not combine unrelated workflows into one broad skill.
Do not use vague descriptions such as general file helper.
Do not run removal commands without verifying the target path.

faq.question: What does this skill help me manage?
faq.answer: It helps create, update, organize, troubleshoot, and share AI agent skills.

faq.question: Does it create files automatically?
faq.answer: It provides commands and structure guidance. File changes depend on the agent tools and user approval flow.

faq.question: Can teams use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains project skill placement and Git sharing patterns for team repositories.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill can use Bash, Edit, and Write, and it includes examples that change local files.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is based on Claude Code style skill files and directories.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. Codex can use the guidance to author or review similar skill documentation.
